title: verbascum glabratum
intro: Verbascum glabratum, also known as woolly mullein, is a biennial plant in the figwort family with large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The leaves and flowers of Verbascum glabratum have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, possessing anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's medicinal properties can be attributed to its saponins and mucilages, which have been used to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and treat various health conditions. Verbascum glabratum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
